title: She Gave Me A Rose
content:
She gave a rose,
    And I kissed it and pressed it.
    I love her, she knows,
    And my action confessed it.
    She gave me a rose,
    And I kissed it and pressed it.

    Ah, how my heart glows,
    Could I ever have guessed it?
    It is fair to suppose
    That I might have repressed it:
    She gave me a rose,
    And I kissed it and pressed it.

    'T was a rhyme in life's prose
    That uplifted and blest it.
    Man's nature, who knows
    Until love comes to test it?
    She gave me a rose,
    And I kissed it and pressed it.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Paul Laurence Dunbar delivers a powerful performance in "She Gave Me A Rose"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
